title: When Walls Come Tumbling Down
story: Every November 9, people around the world remember one of history's most exciting moments - the fall of the Berlin Wall! In 1989, thousands of Germans grabbed hammers, pickaxes, and anything they could find to tear down the concrete wall that had divided their city for 28 years. Many visit Berlin to see the colorful murals painted on remaining pieces of the wall, while others hold festivals celebrating how barriers between people can be broken down through hope and determination.

story: Ringo Starr drummed up excitement in 1973 with his album 'Ringo'. Released in the UK on November 9, a week after its US debut, it became his biggest hit. Fans loved the catchy singles 'Photograph', 'You're Sixteen', and 'Oh My My'. Critics cheered too, making it Ringo's most successful solo project since The Beatles.

activity_answer: Photograph, You're Sixteen, and Oh My My

title: A Musical Bridge to Remember
story: Harmonies filled the air as Simon & Garfunkel stepped into the recording studio. On November 9, 1969, the duo laid down tracks for their soon-to-be iconic single, 'Bridge over Troubled Water'.
